text: Hello, here we see a laparoscopic cholecystectomy to lift the rectum that is filled with rectal stones that cause the upper right abdomen to rupture repeatedly and for quite a long time. Here it looks like there are many joints that surround the rectum, so it must be released to achieve an important structure when performing a cholecystectomy. such as SysTick Duck and SysTick Artery. The bonding can be freed by using an energy device such as a mono-counter that is connected to a fruit in Zubat, Maryland, or by using a harmonic snowball as shown in the video now.

The surgeon carefully dissects to isolate the cystic duct. Once isolated, three clips are applied: two proximally and one distally. The cystic duct is then transected. Blood vessels are cleaned to enhance visibility in the surgical field.
The surgeon isolates the cystic artery during the laparoscopic cholecystectomy, applying clips to secure the artery before transecting it.
